edible_parts: Not eaten raw (toxic); leaves/roots only after thorough drying (historic).
other_uses: Remarkable wetland arum that melts snow with its own heat and blooms in late winter with a hooded spathe.
prep: A fascinating earliest-spring wetland plant (thermogenic); ALL parts are intensely acrid and toxic raw — leaves and roots were eaten only after long drying. Grow it for its ecology, not the table.
soil: Wet, mucky soils; swamps and seeps.
risks: Intensely acrid/toxic raw (calcium oxalate); requires thorough drying to eat.
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
